note: Self-improving loop: improve multicolor pipeline for 20-30 color images; target hw_forest_cat quality.

tried.note: rejected at mt=8 because clean cells had no slack; now kept when combined with mt=4.
saliency bias becomes net-positive when clean cells have slack from mt=4

forest_cat_renders.note: FIRST measurable forest_cat change: +165 paths (+2%) from saliency-weighted init picking different cluster centers

observations: 3 kept changes total: mt 8→6→4, saliency 0→1. Global median 0.2325→0.1899 (-18.3%).

Key finding: single probes are order-dependent. saliencyWeight failed at mt=8 (iter 5) because clean cells already at their limit; kept at mt=4 because clean cells have slack. Future probing should re-test rejected probes after each kept change.
Forest cat finally shifted: 8273→8438 paths (+2%) from the saliency-weighted init. Visual comparison needed to judge if tree-doors improve — path count is a coarse proxy.
Max loss dropped meaningfully: 0.697 → 0.653. The worst noisy cell is getting slightly better without the clean cells regressing.

exit_status.n30_clean_15pct: ACHIEVED (-76% at mt=4; likely similar or better with saliency)
exit_status.forest_cat_distinct: PARTIAL (paths changed +2%; needs visual inspection of tree-door region)

next_probe: Re-test previously-rejected probes under the new best config (mt=4, sal=1): denoisePasses, restarts, autoKStrategy silhouette.
